---
title: '#Excercise3'
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "2026-03-27"
---

#Solution-1

In this point we will draw 1000 realizations from a Gaussian random variable with mean equal to 7 and variance equal to 02

```{r}
set.seed(130)
x <- rnorm(n=1000, mean =7, sd=sqrt(2))

summary(x)
```

We observed that the empirical mean value is very close to the theoretical one (7).  It is also very clear to empirical median, which is a hint of a symmetric distribution. Also the relative position of the first and third quartiles (which are equally spaced with respect to the central point) suggests that the distribution is equally symmetric.

```{r}
sd(x)
```

We now plot the histogram of the distribution. 

```{r}
hist(x, breaks =10, main="Histogram")
```
#ECDF- Empirical Cumulative Distribution Function 

```{r}
plot(ecdf(x), do.points =T, main="ECDF")
```

```{r}
qqnorm(x, main ="QQ-plot")
qqline(x)
```
